MemBench is a temporal disambiguation benchmark where the correct answer depends on
lifecycle state management. It comprises 516 questions across 40 synthetic personas, each with ∼35
multi-session conversations spanning 18 simulated months, with nine attack vectors (Table 2) targeting
distinct lifecycle failure modes. Personas span 14 nationalities, ages 22–68, and diverse occupations,
and are generated conversation-first under strict rules ensuring natural dialogue, accompanied by
structured YAML ground truth enume

When a user says “I moved from Istanbul to London,” the ingestion classifier
tags this as Identity & Self-Concept with slot key (user, current_city). The high-confidence
supersession mechanism marks the earlier “lives in Istanbul” edge as superseded. A subsequent query
“Where does the user live?” triggers the lifecycle

or combined fact extraction and behavioral classification. Retrieval combines cosine similarity
(top-60), BM25 keyword matching (top-20), category-forced retrieval (top-20), and LLM-based
memory distillation (selecting top-20 from the merged pool), followed by the full lifecycle filtering
stack. The only dependencies are openai, numpy, and rank_bm25, with no graph database, no entity
resolution, and no deduplication pipel
